concerns.text: The validation of inconsistency‑detection precision (SC‑002) relies on ‘ground‑truth labels generated by two independent domain experts’. If those experts determine consistency by recomputing p‑values/effect sizes using the same reconstruction logic that the system under test employs, the validation becomes circular: the predictor (reconstructed statistic) and the validation target (expert label) are not independent. This undermines the ability to empirically assess the detector’s performance. The spec must specify an independent source of truth (e.g., raw outcome counts from the original experiment, or a synthetic dataset with known parameters) that is not derived from the system’s own reconstruction pipeline.
